{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "JobPosting",
  "title": "Operations Generalist",
  "description": "Sabaoth Tech Ltd is an innovative IT consultancy firm specializing in software development and infrastructure solutions tailored to diverse client needs. With a commitment to providing comprehensive and customized services, Sabaoth strives to address challenges with creativity and depth. Our mission is centered on delivering value-driven outcomes, as your success is our success. We are dedicated to fostering long-term partnerships by creating impactful technological solutions.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Operations Generalist

Job Location: Ibadan, Oyo
Employment Type: Full-time

About the role


	We&#39;re looking for a versatile operations generalist who thrives on variety and enjoys being the connective tissue that holds our organization together.
	This isn&#39;t a role where you&#39;ll do the same thing every day&mdash;you&#39;ll move fluidly between HR, finance, administration, and operational projects, bringing order to complexity and building deep expertise across our entire operation.
	If you&#39;re the kind of person who gets energized by switching contexts, solving different types of problems, and being the person others turn to when they need something done, this role is for you.


What you&#39;ll do
Human Resources (25%):


	Manage recruitment processes from job posting through onboarding
	Administer employee benefits and maintain HR records
	Handle employee relations matters and serve as a point of contact for HR questions
	Maintain and update policy documentation
	Coordinate training and professional development initiatives


Finance (25%):


	Process invoices, expenses, and vendor payments
	Perform bookkeeping and monthly account reconciliation
	Track budgets and flag variances
	Prepare financial reports and support audit processes
	Manage payroll administration


Administration (25%):


	Coordinate office operations and facilities management
	Manage vendor relationships and negotiate contracts
	Maintain document management systems
	Schedule meetings and manage executive calendars
	Oversee office supplies and equipment procurement


Operations (25%):


	Identify and implement process improvements across departments
	Coordinate cross-functional projects and initiatives
	Maintain operational systems and tools
	Support teams with ad-hoc requests and problem-solving
	Document procedures and create operational playbooks


What we&#39;re looking for
Required:


	2+ years of experience in operations, administration, HR, finance, or a similar support role
	Strong organizational skills with exceptional attention to detail
	Comfort with ambiguity and rapidly changing priorities
	Ability to context-switch between different types of work throughout the day
	Excellent written and verbal communication skills
	Proficiency with standard business software (spreadsheets, documents, email)
	Problem-solving and analytical abilities to address diverse challenges and propose solutions
	Ability to work independently and manage time efficiently in a remote setting


Who thrives in this role:
This position is ideal for someone who:


	Gets bored doing the same thing every day and craves variety
	Enjoys being a generalist rather than a specialist
	Likes being the person who knows how everything works
	Finds satisfaction in keeping systems running smoothly
	Wants to build a broad skill set across multiple business functions
	Doesn&#39;t need clearly defined lane boundaries to feel successful


Why this role matters:


	You&#39;ll become indispensable.
	While specialists go deep in one area, you&#39;ll be the rare person who understands how all our systems connect.
	You&#39;ll see inefficiencies others miss, solve problems that fall between departments, and become the institutional memory that makes our organization run better.


What we offer


	
	Competitive salary commensurate with experience.
	


Method of Application&nbsp;
Interested and qualified candidates should send their resume and a brief note about why variety energizes you to: careers@sabaothcloud.com using &quot;Operations Generalist&quot; as the subject of the mail.

Note: We&#39;re reviewing applications on a rolling basis and will schedule initial conversations with qualified candidates within two weeks of application.",

Operations Generalist Job at Sabaoth Tech Ltd




        
    

        
        Sabaoth Tech Ltd is an innovative IT consultancy firm specializing in software development and infrastructure solutions tailored to diverse client needs. With a commitment to providing comprehensive and customized services, Sabaoth strives to address challenges with creativity and depth. Our mission is centered on delivering value-driven outcomes, as your success is our success. We are dedicated to fostering long-term partnerships by creating impactful technological solutions.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Operations Generalist

Job Location: Ibadan, Oyo
Employment Type: Full-time

About the role
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    




	We're looking for a versatile operations generalist who thrives on variety and enjoys being the connective tissue that holds our organization together.
	This isn't a role where you'll do the same thing every day—you'll move fluidly between HR, finance, administration, and operational projects, bringing order to complexity and building deep expertise across our entire operation.
	If you're the kind of person who gets energized by switching contexts, solving different types of problems, and being the person others turn to when they need something done, this role is for you.


What you'll do
Human Resources (25%):


	Manage recruitment processes from job posting through onboarding
	Administer employee benefits and maintain HR records
	Handle employee relations matters and serve as a point of contact for HR questions
	Maintain and update policy documentation
	Coordinate training and professional development initiatives


Finance (25%):


	Process invoices, expenses, and vendor payments
	Perform bookkeeping and monthly account reconciliation
	Track budgets and flag variances
	Prepare financial reports and support audit processes
	Manage payroll administration


Administration (25%):


	Coordinate office operations and facilities management
	Manage vendor relationships and negotiate contracts
	Maintain document management systems
	Schedule meetings and manage executive calendars
	Oversee office supplies and equipment procurement


Operations (25%):


	Identify and implement process improvements across departments
	Coordinate cross-functional projects and initiatives
	Maintain operational systems and tools
	Support teams with ad-hoc requests and problem-solving
	Document procedures and create operational playbooks


What we're looking for
Required:


	2+ years of experience in operations, administration, HR, finance, or a similar support role
	Strong organizational skills with exceptional attention to detail
	Comfort with ambiguity and rapidly changing priorities
	Ability to context-switch between different types of work throughout the day
	Excellent written and verbal communication skills
	Proficiency with standard business software (spreadsheets, documents, email)
	Problem-solving and analytical abilities to address diverse challenges and propose solutions
	Ability to work independently and manage time efficiently in a remote setting


Who thrives in this role:
This position is ideal for someone who:


	Gets bored doing the same thing every day and craves variety
	Enjoys being a generalist rather than a specialist
	Likes being the person who knows how everything works
	Finds satisfaction in keeping systems running smoothly
	Wants to build a broad skill set across multiple business functions
	Doesn't need clearly defined lane boundaries to feel successful


Why this role matters:


	You'll become indispensable.
	While specialists go deep in one area, you'll be the rare person who understands how all our systems connect.
	You'll see inefficiencies others miss, solve problems that fall between departments, and become the institutional memory that makes our organization run better.


What we offer


	
	Competitive salary commensurate with experience.
	


Method of Application 
Interested and qualified candidates should send their resume and a brief note about why variety energizes you to: [email protected] using "Operations Generalist" as the subject of the mail.

Note: We're reviewing applications on a rolling basis and will schedule initial conversations with qualified candidates within two weeks of application.    

    

        Click here to Apply

{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "JobPosting",
  "title": "Executive Assistant & Event Coordinator to the CEO",
  "description": "Amala Ibadan was founded on a simple yet powerful premise: that traditional and contemprorary Nigerian meals should be accessible, high-quality, and served in a professional environment. Since our inception, we have transitioned from a single-location concept to a multi-outlet enterprise, standardizing the art of local cooking to meet corporate-level service benchmarks.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Executive Assistant &amp; Event Coordinator to the CEO

Job Location: Lagos&nbsp;
Employment Type: Full-time

Role Overview


	The Executive Assistant &amp; Event Coordinator to the CEO is a pivotal role at the center of the organization&rsquo;s leadership operations.
	This position provides end-to-end support to the CEO by managing priorities, streamlining workflows, and anticipating needs in a fast-paced environment.
	Beyond executive support, the role is instrumental in planning and delivering high-profile meetings, engagements, and events that reflect the CEO&rsquo;s vision and the organization&rsquo;s standards.
	The ideal candidate is exceptionally organized, forward-thinking, highly resourceful, and trusted with sensitive information. Acting as a key point of connection between the CEO and both internal teams and external partners, this role ensures clear communication, operational efficiency, and seamless execution across administrative and event-related functions.


Key Responsibilities
Executive &amp; Administrative Support:


	Proactively manage the CEO&rsquo;s schedule, coordinating meetings, appointments, and commitments with a strong focus on urgency, priorities, and strategic impact.
	Act as the central contact for the CEO&rsquo;s office, handling calls, emails, and correspondence with professionalism, discretion, and sound judgment.
	Develop, review, and structure executive-level reports, presentations, briefs, and key documentation to support informed decision-making.
	Establish and maintain well-organized filing systems, databases, and records, ensuring accuracy, accessibility, and strict confidentiality.


Communication &amp; Stakeholder Management:


	Serve as a trusted gatekeeper, assessing, prioritizing, and routing communications to ensure efficiency and focus.
	Draft clear, professional correspondence and official communications on behalf of the CEO.
	Facilitate seamless communication between the CEO and internal teams, external partners, vendors, and key stakeholders.


Meetings &amp; Travel Coordination:


	Plan and coordinate executive meetings end-to-end, including agenda development, logistics, minute-taking, action tracking, and follow-ups.
	Oversee all travel arrangements, managing flights, accommodation, ground transportation, and comprehensive itineraries to ensure smooth execution.


Event Planning &amp; Brand Engagement:


	Lead the planning and execution of corporate events, brand activations, meetings, conferences, and special appearances.
	Manage vendors, venues, logistics, and timelines to deliver seamless events that reflect and strengthen the Amala Ibadan brand.
	Provide support for select personal or social events connected to the CEO&rsquo;s role, as required.


Research &amp; Strategic Assistance:


	Conduct targeted research, synthesize insights, and prepare briefs to inform executive decisions.
	Support special projects, initiatives, and time-sensitive assignments delegated by the CEO.


Personal &amp; Lifestyle Coordination:


	Handle designated personal tasks and errands for the CEO with discretion, efficiency, and professionalism.
	Coordinate household or personal staff and manage ad-hoc personal requests when necessary.


Essential Skills &amp; Competencies


	Strong organizational and time-management abilities with exceptional attention to detail.
	Excellent verbal and written communication skills.
	Proactive, solution-oriented mindset with the ability to anticipate needs and work independently.
	High standards of integrity, discretion, and confidentiality.
	Adaptability and composure in a fast-paced, evolving environment.
	Proficiency in productivity tools, scheduling platforms, and digital record-keeping systems.


Preferred Qualifications:


	Prior experience supporting a senior executive, founder, or business owner.
	Interest or background in hospitality, food brands, or event management is an added advantage.
	Polished, professional presence with the ability to represent the CEO and brand confidently.


Method of Application&nbsp;
Interested and qualified candidates should send their applications to: hr@amalaibadan.ng using the Job Position as the subject of the mail.",

  "validThrough": "2026-06-13",

Executive Assistant & Event Coordinator to the CEO Job at Amala Ibadan




        
    

        
        Amala Ibadan was founded on a simple yet powerful premise: that traditional and contemprorary Nigerian meals should be accessible, high-quality, and served in a professional environment. Since our inception, we have transitioned from a single-location concept to a multi-outlet enterprise, standardizing the art of local cooking to meet corporate-level service benchmarks.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Executive Assistant & Event Coordinator to the CEO

Job Location: Lagos 
Employment Type: Full-time

Role Overview
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    




	The Executive Assistant & Event Coordinator to the CEO is a pivotal role at the center of the organization’s leadership operations.
	This position provides end-to-end support to the CEO by managing priorities, streamlining workflows, and anticipating needs in a fast-paced environment.
	Beyond executive support, the role is instrumental in planning and delivering high-profile meetings, engagements, and events that reflect the CEO’s vision and the organization’s standards.
	The ideal candidate is exceptionally organized, forward-thinking, highly resourceful, and trusted with sensitive information. Acting as a key point of connection between the CEO and both internal teams and external partners, this role ensures clear communication, operational efficiency, and seamless execution across administrative and event-related functions.


Key Responsibilities
Executive & Administrative Support:


	Proactively manage the CEO’s schedule, coordinating meetings, appointments, and commitments with a strong focus on urgency, priorities, and strategic impact.
	Act as the central contact for the CEO’s office, handling calls, emails, and correspondence with professionalism, discretion, and sound judgment.
	Develop, review, and structure executive-level reports, presentations, briefs, and key documentation to support informed decision-making.
	Establish and maintain well-organized filing systems, databases, and records, ensuring accuracy, accessibility, and strict confidentiality.


Communication & Stakeholder Management:


	Serve as a trusted gatekeeper, assessing, prioritizing, and routing communications to ensure efficiency and focus.
	Draft clear, professional correspondence and official communications on behalf of the CEO.
	Facilitate seamless communication between the CEO and internal teams, external partners, vendors, and key stakeholders.


Meetings & Travel Coordination:


	Plan and coordinate executive meetings end-to-end, including agenda development, logistics, minute-taking, action tracking, and follow-ups.
	Oversee all travel arrangements, managing flights, accommodation, ground transportation, and comprehensive itineraries to ensure smooth execution.


Event Planning & Brand Engagement:


	Lead the planning and execution of corporate events, brand activations, meetings, conferences, and special appearances.
	Manage vendors, venues, logistics, and timelines to deliver seamless events that reflect and strengthen the Amala Ibadan brand.
	Provide support for select personal or social events connected to the CEO’s role, as required.


Research & Strategic Assistance:


	Conduct targeted research, synthesize insights, and prepare briefs to inform executive decisions.
	Support special projects, initiatives, and time-sensitive assignments delegated by the CEO.


Personal & Lifestyle Coordination:


	Handle designated personal tasks and errands for the CEO with discretion, efficiency, and professionalism.
	Coordinate household or personal staff and manage ad-hoc personal requests when necessary.


Essential Skills & Competencies


	Strong organizational and time-management abilities with exceptional attention to detail.
	Excellent verbal and written communication skills.
	Proactive, solution-oriented mindset with the ability to anticipate needs and work independently.
	High standards of integrity, discretion, and confidentiality.
	Adaptability and composure in a fast-paced, evolving environment.
	Proficiency in productivity tools, scheduling platforms, and digital record-keeping systems.


Preferred Qualifications:


	Prior experience supporting a senior executive, founder, or business owner.
	Interest or background in hospitality, food brands, or event management is an added advantage.
	Polished, professional presence with the ability to represent the CEO and brand confidently.


Method of Application 
Interested and qualified candidates should send their applications to: [email protected] using the Job Position as the subject of the mail.

{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "JobPosting",
  "title": "Enterprise Risk Management Officer",
  "description": "Sovereign Finance Limited is a leading financial services provider with a prime desire to promote trailblazing Financial &amp; Investment solutions to customers in different sectors of the Nigerian economy. Our priority is to create opportunities for our clients to save accumulated capital and build sustainable wealth. The company is founded by a team of savvy finance &amp; investment specialist with evidence of track record of exceptional performance, superb integrity, and innovation. The company is at the center of financial innovation propelled by technological prowess and is at a position of pre-eminence in the Nigeria financial Industry.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Enterprise Risk Management Officer

Job Location: Lagos&nbsp;
Employment Type: Full-time

Summary


	The Enterprise Risk Management Officer will be responsible for identifying, assessing, monitoring, and mitigating risks across various Units of the Bank.


Key Responsibilities


	Assess the Bank&rsquo;s core risk areas and recommend appropriate mitigation measures.
	Manage and monitor risk exposures across various units, ensuring compliance with internal policies and regulatory expectations.
	Create, analyze, and disseminate risk reports and dashboards for internal control and senior management review.
	Follow up on compliance issues and audit observations, ensuring timely closure and control remediation.
	Review and escalate findings from customer complaints received via operation or CX , and support investigations in collaboration with the Unit Head.
	Conduct daily transaction checks and exception reports, especially those related to GL postings, operations and loan disbursement.
	Perform transaction call-over, quality control checks, and loan officer activity reviews.
	Support the documentation and continuous improvement of the Bank&rsquo;s Enterprise Risk Management (ERM) framework.
	Participate in periodic risk assessments and stress testing.
	Carry out other ad hoc risk-related assignments as directed by the Unit Head


Qualifications &amp; Requirements


	Bachelor&rsquo;s degree in Finance, Risk Management, Accounting, Economics, or related field.
	3- 4 years of relevant experience in risk, compliance, internal control, preferably within a microfinance or retail banking institution.
	Familiarity with risk frameworks such as Risk Management Graduation Model (RMGM), and a strong understanding of CBN/NDIC regulatory requirements.
	Proficiency in risk reporting, issue tracking, and operational control monitoring.
	Excellent communication and report-writing skills.
	Strong ethical judgment and attention to detail.


Why Work with Liquid Crest Mfb


	Salary: N350,000 &ndash; N400,000 monthly.
	Comprehensive HMO coverage
	Continuous training and professional development
	Leave allowance
	Clear career growth opportunities in a mission-driven institution
	Supportive work environment where performance and passion are rewarded.


Method of Application&nbsp;
Intrested and qualified candidates should send their CV to: careers@sovereignfinanceltd.com using the Job Position as the subject of the email.",

  },

      "addressCountry": "NG"

Enterprise Risk Management Officer Job at Sovereign Finance Limited




        
    

        
        Sovereign Finance Limited is a leading financial services provider with a prime desire to promote trailblazing Financial & Investment solutions to customers in different sectors of the Nigerian economy. Our priority is to create opportunities for our clients to save accumulated capital and build sustainable wealth. The company is founded by a team of savvy finance & investment specialist with evidence of track record of exceptional performance, superb integrity, and innovation. The company is at the center of financial innovation propelled by technological prowess and is at a position of pre-eminence in the Nigeria financial Industry.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Enterprise Risk Management Officer
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    



Job Location: Lagos 
Employment Type: Full-time

Summary


	The Enterprise Risk Management Officer will be responsible for identifying, assessing, monitoring, and mitigating risks across various Units of the Bank.


Key Responsibilities


	Assess the Bank’s core risk areas and recommend appropriate mitigation measures.
	Manage and monitor risk exposures across various units, ensuring compliance with internal policies and regulatory expectations.
	Create, analyze, and disseminate risk reports and dashboards for internal control and senior management review.
	Follow up on compliance issues and audit observations, ensuring timely closure and control remediation.
	Review and escalate findings from customer complaints received via operation or CX , and support investigations in collaboration with the Unit Head.
	Conduct daily transaction checks and exception reports, especially those related to GL postings, operations and loan disbursement.
	Perform transaction call-over, quality control checks, and loan officer activity reviews.
	Support the documentation and continuous improvement of the Bank’s Enterprise Risk Management (ERM) framework.
	Participate in periodic risk assessments and stress testing.
	Carry out other ad hoc risk-related assignments as directed by the Unit Head


Qualifications & Requirements


	Bachelor’s degree in Finance, Risk Management, Accounting, Economics, or related field.
	3- 4 years of relevant experience in risk, compliance, internal control, preferably within a microfinance or retail banking institution.
	Familiarity with risk frameworks such as Risk Management Graduation Model (RMGM), and a strong understanding of CBN/NDIC regulatory requirements.
	Proficiency in risk reporting, issue tracking, and operational control monitoring.
	Excellent communication and report-writing skills.
	Strong ethical judgment and attention to detail.


Why Work with Liquid Crest Mfb


	Salary: N350,000 – N400,000 monthly.
	Comprehensive HMO coverage
	Continuous training and professional development
	Leave allowance
	Clear career growth opportunities in a mission-driven institution
	Supportive work environment where performance and passion are rewarded.


Method of Application 
Intrested and qualified candidates should send their CV to: [email protected] using the Job Position as the subject of the email.    

    

        Click here to Apply

  "description": "Carrot-Top Drugs Limited was incorporated in 1997. It started business as a retail pharmacy on October 30th, 2000. In 2005, the company started her own brands of innovative and quality fertility products with Predict Ovulation Prediction Kit being her first.

Today, Carrot-Top Drugs Limited is a household name for couples trying for a baby. The company is built on a tripod of hard work, transparency, and commitment to our numerous customers. Our passion for fertility and wellness is borne out of our desire to see couples fulfilled and happy. Our various communication channels are inundated with testimonies of obviously satisfied customers. Our strength is in our skilled, courteous, and very innovative manpower, as well as our distribution.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Area Manager

Job Location: Lagos Mainland, Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time

Requirements


	Candidates must possess a Degree in any of the following disciplines: Human Physiology, Anatomy, Pharmacology, Biochemistry, Microbiology or Biological Sciences.
	Must be resident in Lagos.
	Must be have at least three years exprience Managing Reps in this AREA
	Will be resposible for all Sales Rep in Lagos and Ogun states.


Method of Application&nbsp;
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to: pharmarecruiter12@gmail.com using the Job Position as the subject of the email.",

Area Manager Job at Carrot-Top Drugs Limited




        
    

        
        Carrot-Top Drugs Limited was incorporated in 1997. It started business as a retail pharmacy on October 30th, 2000. In 2005, the company started her own brands of innovative and quality fertility products with Predict Ovulation Prediction Kit being her first.

Today, Carrot-Top Drugs Limited is a household name for couples trying for a baby. The company is built on a tripod of hard work, transparency, and commitment to our numerous customers. Our passion for fertility and wellness is borne out of our desire to see couples fulfilled and happy. Our various communication channels are inundated with testimonies of obviously satisfied customers. Our strength is in our skilled, courteous, and very innovative manpower, as well as our distribution.
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    



We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Area Manager

Job Location: Lagos Mainland, Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time

Requirements


	Candidates must possess a Degree in any of the following disciplines: Human Physiology, Anatomy, Pharmacology, Biochemistry, Microbiology or Biological Sciences.
	Must be resident in Lagos.
	Must be have at least three years exprience Managing Reps in this AREA
	Will be resposible for all Sales Rep in Lagos and Ogun states.


Method of Application 
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to: [email protected] using the Job Position as the subject of the email.

  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "JobPosting",
  "title": "Warranty Officer",
  "description": "Dana is a conglomerate with diversified business interest in different sectors of the economy.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Warranty Officer

Job Location: Isolo, Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time

Job Description
Key Responsibilities:


	Provide administrative and operational support to the workshop on all warranty jobs (PWA).
	Verify warranty eligibility before approving repairs or responding to warranty-related inquiries.
	Respond promptly to emails and warranty requests from other branches after confirming eligibility.
	Collect warranty invoices from the Accounts Department for processing and record-keeping.
	Retrieve old warranty parts from the workshop and ensure they are properly stored in the designated warranty store.
	Maintain the warranty store room in a clean, organized, and audit-ready condition.
	Prepare and submit weekly reports detailing all warranty jobs completed.
	Maintain accurate warranty records and documentation for audit and compliance purposes.
	Ensure proper tracking of warranty parts returned and claims submitted.
	Liaise with workshop supervisors and service advisors to ensure timely processing of warranty claims.


Qualifications &amp; Experience


	Minimum of OND / HND / Bachelor&rsquo;s Degree in Business Administration, Accounting, or related fields
	1&ndash;3 years of experience in warranty administration, workshop operations, or automotive after-sales support
	Basic understanding of automotive workshop processes is an advantage
	Proficiency in Microsoft Excel and email communication
	Good organizational and record-keeping skills, Basic accounting knowledge and Effective communication skills.


Method of Application
Interested and qualified candidates should forward their CV to: jobs@kiamotorsnigeria.com using the Job Position as the subject of the mail.",

Warranty Officer Job at Dana




        
    

        
        Dana is a conglomerate with diversified business interest in different sectors of the economy.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Warranty Officer

Job Location: Isolo, Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time

Job Description
Key Responsibilities:
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    




	Provide administrative and operational support to the workshop on all warranty jobs (PWA).
	Verify warranty eligibility before approving repairs or responding to warranty-related inquiries.
	Respond promptly to emails and warranty requests from other branches after confirming eligibility.
	Collect warranty invoices from the Accounts Department for processing and record-keeping.
	Retrieve old warranty parts from the workshop and ensure they are properly stored in the designated warranty store.
	Maintain the warranty store room in a clean, organized, and audit-ready condition.
	Prepare and submit weekly reports detailing all warranty jobs completed.
	Maintain accurate warranty records and documentation for audit and compliance purposes.
	Ensure proper tracking of warranty parts returned and claims submitted.
	Liaise with workshop supervisors and service advisors to ensure timely processing of warranty claims.


Qualifications & Experience


	Minimum of OND / HND / Bachelor’s Degree in Business Administration, Accounting, or related fields
	1–3 years of experience in warranty administration, workshop operations, or automotive after-sales support
	Basic understanding of automotive workshop processes is an advantage
	Proficiency in Microsoft Excel and email communication
	Good organizational and record-keeping skills, Basic accounting knowledge and Effective communication skills.


Method of Application
Interested and qualified candidates should forward their CV to: [email protected] using the Job Position as the subject of the mail.
